SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.spi

Class EntityCacheImpl<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.spi.EntityCacheImpl<E,ID>
Type Parameters:
E - the entity type.
ID - the primary key type.
All Implemented Interfaces:
EntityCache<E,ID>
public final class EntityCacheImpl<E extends Entity<ID>,ID> extends Object implements EntityCache<E,ID>
Transaction-local cache that interns entities by primary key with configurable retention behavior.

This cache ensures that, within a transaction, logically identical entities are represented by a single object instance whenever possible. Observed entity state is retained for dirty checking as long as the application holds a reference to the entity, and may be cleaned up automatically when the entity is no longer referenced.

Cache entries are cleaned up lazily when new entries are interned.

Retention configuration

The retention behavior can be configured via the storm.entity_cache.retention property (see StormConfig):

  • default (default): Observed state is retained for the duration of the transaction, improving the dirty-check hit rate at the cost of higher memory usage during transactions.
  • light: Observed state may be cleaned up as soon as the application no longer holds a reference to the entity. This reduces memory overhead.

Interning semantics

  • Entities are indexed by their primary key.
  • If an entity with the same primary key is already cached and still alive, it is returned only if it is considered equal according to Object.equals(Object).
  • If the cached instance differs logically (i.e. equals returns false), the cache is updated to reference the newly provided instance.

This behavior provides identity stability for logically identical data while still permitting newer or different representations of the same primary key to replace older ones.

Thread-safety

This implementation is not thread-safe. It is intended for use as a transaction-scoped cache accessed by a single thread.

Since:
1.7

  • Constructor Details

    • EntityCacheImpl

      public EntityCacheImpl(@Nonnull CacheRetention retention)
      Creates a new entity cache with the specified retention behavior.
      Parameters:
      retention - the cache retention strategy to use.

  • Method Details

    • get

      public Optional<E> get(@Nonnull ID pk)
      Retrieves an entity from the cache by primary key, if available.

      The returned entity is the currently cached instance for the given primary key if it is still reachable. A previously cached entity may have been cleaned up and therefore no longer be available.

      Specified by:
      get in interface EntityCache<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>
      Parameters:
      pk - the primary key to look up.
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the cached entity if present and still alive, or Optional.empty() otherwise.

    • intern

      public E intern(@Nonnull E entity)
      Returns a canonical instance for the given entity within this cache.

      If an entity with the same primary key is already cached and still reachable, that instance is returned only if it is considered logically equal according to Object.equals(Object). In that case, the existing cached instance is reused.

      If no cached instance exists, the cached instance has been cleaned up, or the cached instance is not equal to the provided entity, the cache is updated to reference the provided entity and that instance is returned.

      Within this cache, for a given primary key, logically identical entities are represented by a single Java object whenever possible.

      Specified by:
      intern in interface EntityCache<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>
      Parameters:
      entity - the entity to intern.
      Returns:
      the canonical cached instance for the entity's primary key.

    • remove

      public void remove(@Nonnull ID pk)
      Removes the cached entry for the given primary key, if present.

      If an entity instance for pk is currently cached, the mapping is removed. If the instance has already been cleaned up, the mapping may be removed either by this call or later via lazy cleanup.

      Specified by:
      remove in interface EntityCache<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>
      Parameters:
      pk - the primary key to remove; must not be null.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all cached mappings.

      This removes all primary key mappings immediately. Any already-enqueued references are harmless; future cleanup will simply find no matching entries to remove.

      Specified by:
      clear in interface EntityCache<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>
